summary: Do not ship the plan yet. The timeout budget breaks as APPS grows, and the promised landing-time evidence has no executable recording point. No materially simpler safe design was found; the rejected r7 transition-test finding is not re-raised.

findings.title: Sequential scrape timeouts exceed the fixed Job deadline
findings.body: The exporter iterates APPS sequentially, while create-app can add entries without a cardinality limit. Four blackholed registries consume 4×30s before the 30s curl allowance, exceeding the 120s Job deadline; three already leave no margin if curl also stalls. The Job can therefore die before publishing heartbeat and count metrics, misreporting a GHCR outage as DigestExporterStale/KubeJobFailed instead of DigestExporterScrapeIncomplete and violating User Story 2. The planned immediate stubs and static flag checks cannot expose this.
findings.recommendation: Define and enforce a maximum APPS cardinality with a deadline inequality that includes every sequential scrape, curl, and margin, or bound total collection time through shorter/parallel queries. Add a timed producer test at the supported cardinality limit.

findings.title: Compensating landing evidence has no executable pipeline step
findings.body: The plan earlier states that Victoria Stack deploys only main and landing occurs after verification/release and ends the flow. It nevertheless requires post-sync successor UID and first-heartbeat latency to be recorded in verification. Before landing, the branch is not deployed; after landing, updating the committed verification artifact would require another change and invalidate release freshness. A competent engineer cannot satisfy this compensating control as written. This does not require reinstating the rejected r7 transition test.
findings.recommendation: Specify an executable finishing/post-merge checkpoint: where the evidence is stored without modifying the reviewed branch, exact pre-merge and post-sync commands, timeout/failure handling, and whether failed evidence blocks or triggers rollback. Otherwise explicitly remove it as a required compensating control and rely on the recorded waiver.

next_steps: Reconcile the APPS cardinality, per-command timeouts, and Job deadline in both the manifest contract and tests.
Define the durable post-landing evidence workflow, then rerun the plan review.
